items.title: Bogus Purchase Addition Remanded Due to Pending HC Decision on Similar Issue
items.summary:
The issue involved additions based on alleged bogus purchases without VAT payment. The Tribunal remanded the matter pending the High Court’s decision on similar cases.

items.title: Section 194-IA TDS on Full Property Value Mandatory, Not Linked to Payment Timing: ITAT Bangalore
items.summary:
The Tribunal upheld TDS liability where tax was deducted only on part of the property value. It ruled that TDS must be applied on the entire consideration for immovable property transactions.

items.title: 80G Approval Allowed as No Profit Motive Found in Micro-Credit Activities: ITAT Chennai
items.summary:
The Tribunal held that activities linked to micro-credit and welfare schemes do not negate charitable status in absence of profit motive. The ruling directs grant of 80G approval based on genuine charitable objectives.

items.title: ITAT Rebukes CIT(A) for Ignoring Tribunal Order, Directs Fresh Adjudication
items.summary:
Tribunal held that non-compliance with earlier appellate directions requires fresh adjudication. Key takeaway: appellate authorities must follow binding instructions.

items.title: Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam
items.summary:
The Tribunal ruled that exemption under Section 11(2) should not be denied solely due to delayed filing of Form-10. It directed reconsideration where the form was available during assessment.

items.title: SC Upholds Quashing of Reassessment Notices Due to Unproven PE Allegations
items.summary:
The Supreme Court dismissed SLPs and upheld the High Court’s finding that reassessment notices lacked tangible material. The ruling reinforces that mere survey findings cannot justify reopening of assessments.

items.title: Calcutta HC Dismisses Tax Appeal Below ₹2 Crore Limit for Failure to Show CBDT Exception Applies
items.summary:
Court found that the appellant failed to clearly identify which exceptional clause applied. Since the tax effect was below Rs.2 crores and no valid exception was demonstrated, the Court held that there was no reason to entertain the appeal. Accordingly, the appeal and the connected application were dismissed.

items.title: ITAT Remands Appeal for Fresh Hearing, Imposes ₹20,000 Cost
items.summary:
The Tribunal set aside orders where the assessee failed to appear and granted another opportunity. It emphasized adherence to natural justice while imposing a cost.

items.title: Reopening of Assessment Upheld Due to Form 26AS Showing Undisclosed Contract Income
items.summary:
The Tribunal upheld reopening under Section 147 as Form 26AS reflected substantial contract receipts despite no return being filed. It ruled that such information constitutes valid grounds for belief of income escaping assessment.

items.title: SC Dismisses Revenue SLP Due to Delay, Upholds Depreciation on Leased Assets
items.summary:
The Supreme Court rejected the appeal due to unexplained delay, leaving the High Court ruling intact. The decision confirms that depreciation is allowable where leased assets are used in business.

items.title: Bombay HC allowed Depreciation on Leased Assets as Business Use Requirement Satisfied
items.summary:
The court held that leased assets qualify for depreciation since they are used in the course of business. It clarified that physical use by the assessee is not required if leasing generates business income.

items.title: Important Changes Under Income Tax Act 2025 and 1961
items.summary:
The new law keeps depreciation methods unchanged for continuity. However, it broadens tax scope by including assets “belonging to” taxpayers beyond legal ownership.

items.title: TDS Payment Under New Income Tax Act 2025 – All You Need to Know
items.summary:

items.title: Tax Treatment of Dealer Benefits Under Section 194R & Income Tax Act 2025
items.summary:
Dealer incentives like tour packages are taxable as business income under the new law. If TDS is borne by the payer, it must be added as additional income through grossing up.

items.title: Section 68 Additions: Favourable Remand Report Shields Assessee
items.summary:

items.title: Section 153C Valid but Addition Fails: No Incriminating Material = No Deemed Dividend

items.title: Reassessment After 4 Years Quashed as No Failure to Disclose Material Facts: Bombay HC

items.title: Penalty U/s 270A Quashed: No Specific Charge of “Misreporting” = No Penalty

items.title: Gauhati HC on a humane approach & powers of Commissioner under Section 264
items.summary:

items.title: Section 234E TDS Late Fee Invalid for Pre-2015 Period Due to Lack of Legal Provision

items.title: Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose
items.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that companies owning and operating channels cannot be compared with distribution company for the purpose of transfer pricing. Accordingly, directs TPO to exclude such comparables for benchmarking distribution segment and determination of ALP.
